Initializing the Xbox Live Library

Before your title can use any of the services provided by Xbox Live, it must initialize the Xbox Live library by calling the XOnlineStartup function. This function invokes the XNetStartup function to initialize the Xbox Secure Network Library. It also calls WSAStartup to start the Xbox Winsock layer. When XOnlineStartup calls these two functions, it passes them a set of default parameters values that work correctly for the vast majority of games. However, if your game requires non-default XNetStartup or WSAStartup, it should call those functions prior to invoking XOnlineStartup.

Before then end, Xbox titles should also shut the libraries down properly by calling the XOnlineCleanup function.
